name: Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive hands-free

parameters.subject: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.
parameters.bodyContent: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.

parameters.text: =✅ Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive hands-free — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 17:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Airtable)

### Export All Rows (Airtable)

Searches Airtable's 'Payloads' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Serialize to CSV · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Backup Size Sane? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Sort Payloads Newest First · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Sort Payloads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive hands-free

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive hands-free. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual webhooks is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Export All Rows (Airtable) → Serialize to CSV → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Sort Payloads Newest First → Cap Batch Volume → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating webhooks in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The empty-backup check exists because a backup that silently 'succeeds' with zero rows is worse than an obvious failure — it gives false confidence right up until the day you actually need to restore. Compression runs before upload, not after, cutting both transfer time and storage footprint in the same step.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Point the export at an empty table on purpose and confirm the alarm fires.
2. Turn off compression and compare the resulting backup file size.
3. Add a retention step that deletes backups older than N days from the destination.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your webhooks process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
